rag_rules: Preserve existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place component type annexes in valid component type scopes
Place implementation annexes in valid implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity when modifying AADL models
Ensure annex blocks are placed in valid scopes according to AADL rules
Use only visible identifiers in AGREE expressions to maintain traceability
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless properly represented
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Avoid placing assumptions and guarantees that refer to component ports in invalid scopes
